Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-10-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lisa L. Oberg, Esq. of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
Lance R. Stewart, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party David Wolgamott

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication is now, per the request of the moving party, solely a motion for summary adjudication on plaintiff's request for punitive damages. The motion for summary adjudication on punitive damages is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Defendant's evidentiary objections 1-5 are sustained. Assuming without deciding that the excerpts from the workers' compensation claims are admissible against authentication objections, those excerpts are not relevant absent information about the facts and circumstances of the workers involved including but not limited to the frequency of their exposure to asbestos-containing materials. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
